description: Write SETUP.md: ordered quickstart covering install Ollama, pull models, set required env vars, run validate.py, start orchestrator_main.py, start agents/orchestrator_bot.py, and add zshrc o alias. Single document, no cross-file hunting.
rationale: Newcomer review: setup steps are spread across 4 files. New-machine setup has no single document to follow.

description: Fix _auto_commit() in executor.py to stage only files listed in files_written (git add <file1> <file2> ...) instead of git add -A, preventing untracked scratch files or Jacob's manual edits from being silently committed alongside AI output
rationale: Skeptic review: git add -A will commit any unrelated changes in the repo at execution time — scratch files, notes, partial edits. Scoped staging is the correct behavior.

description: Add CONTEXT.md staleness notification: if update_context_md() returns False (Ollama returned nothing useful), post to #orchestrator-live 'Context update failed for [project] — next task will run against stale context' so Jacob knows the feedback loop broke
rationale: AI engineer review: silent CONTEXT.md update failures mean MiniMax generates future tasks against outdated state. The failure is logged but not surfaced where Jacob will see it.

description: Add confirmation step for bulk Discord approvals in agents/orchestrator_bot.py: 'approve all' and 'approve everything' must reply with task count and wait for a 'confirm' follow-up within 60 seconds before executing, using a pending_confirmation dict keyed by channel ID
rationale: Security review: bulk approve with no confirmation is one mistyped message away from approving the entire queue. Low implementation cost, high safety value.

description: Add COUNCIL_TEMPERATURE constant to config.py (default 0.75) and CFG dict, then replace the hardcoded 0.75 in task_generator.py _call_perspective() with CFG['COUNCIL_TEMPERATURE'] so it's tunable without touching source code
rationale: AI engineer review: council temperature is the only model parameter not in config.py. Should be adjustable alongside OLLAMA_MODEL_CODE and other model settings.

description: Move REPO_PATHS in config.py from hardcoded absolute paths to environment variables or a gitignored .env.local file (loaded via python-dotenv or manual parsing), so the repo works on any machine without editing config.py
rationale: Newcomer review: config.py hardcodes Jacob's Mac home paths, making it non-portable. Anyone cloning this (or Jacob on a new machine) must manually hunt and edit paths before anything runs.

description: Create com.jacob.orchestrator.plist launchd file that auto-starts orchestrator_main.py on macOS login, restarts on crash, and writes stdout/stderr to logs/orchestrator.log. Include launchctl load/unload commands in a comment header.
rationale: Engineering architect review: orchestrator_main.py has no crash recovery. If it dies at 3am it stays dead. Launchd is the macOS-native solution with zero new dependencies.

description: Implement FEAT-3 rejection feedback loop: (1) after Discord 'reject <id>' command, ask for optional reason via follow-up and call task_queue.mark_rejected(task, reason), (2) in task_generator._call_perspective() load last 5 rejections for the project and append them to the system prompt as 'Recent rejections to avoid:', (3) in _select_perspectives() deprioritize perspectives with rejection rate >30% from metrics_data()
rationale: FEAT-4 metrics are now in place providing the rejection rate signal. Council currently has no memory of what Jacob rejected — it will re-propose the same bad patterns indefinitely without this loop.
